---
layout: post
title: "An assault on mediocrity"
date: 2015-04-16
---

<br />
<div style="-en-clipboard: true; -en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em;">
<a href="https://www.blogger.com/blogger.g?blogID=9145285415158126914" imageanchor="1" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;"></a>With the appointment of two energetic heads of teaching and learning this year, we have begun a determined assault on mediocrity in the classroom. The mantra this term has been to get pupils - of whatever ability - into their respective 'struggle zones':</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em;">
<span style="font-weight: bold;"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," style="display: block; height: auto; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;" /></span></div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em;">
Despite having recently redrafted our <a href="https://docs.google.com/document/d/1hS8WAa0GTHwQ9ATa4d1oR_9cEIOdv15m3bxWKpFSGLg/edit" shape="rect">Gifted and Talented Policy&nbsp;</a>we are slowly coming to the realisation that labelling pupils in this way may be unhelpful. Sure, we want the best to be stretched, but what we really want is for <span style="font-style: italic;">everyone to</span> be <span style="font-style: italic;">stretched</span>. One of the dangers with bolt-ons like a Gifted and Talented Policy is that it can divert attention from longer-term and more deep-rooted solutions to getting the very best out of pupils <span style="font-style: italic;">right across the school.&nbsp;</span></div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em;">
Our aim must be, surely, to make sure that in all areas of the school, by whatever means are felt necessary by the resident experts, pupils are stretched and able to reach their full potential. This is easier said than done, of course, but the poem below, which I stumbled across on <a href="https://classteaching.wordpress.com/" shape="rect">Shaun Allison's blog</a> highlights just how important it is:</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<span style="font-weight: bold;">An average child</span></div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
I don’t cause teachers trouble,<br />
My grades have been O.K.<br />
I listen to my classes.<br />
I’m in school every day.</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<br clear="none" />
My parents think I’m average,<br />
My teachers think so, too.<br />
I wish I didn’t know that<br />
’Cause there’s lots I’d like to do.</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<br clear="none" />
I’d like to build a rocket,<br />
I’ve a book that shows you how;<br />
Or start a stamp collection –<br />
Well, no use starting now.</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<br clear="none" />
’Cause since I found I’m average<br />
I’m just smart enough to see,<br />
To know there’s nothing special<br />
That I should expect of me.</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<br clear="none" />
I’m part of that majority,<br />
That hump part of the bell,<br />
Who spends his life unnoticed<br />
In an average kind of hell.</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
</div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em; padding-left: 90px;">
<span style="font-style: italic;">By Mike Buscemi</span></div>
<div style="-en-paragraph: true; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-top: 1em;">
A sobering reminder for us all.</div>
